## Ashwin's Internal Notes - PASS (FYI since EF) - 2026-02-27
Pass for me. CTO who's surrounded himself with two other technical cofounders and unproven on GTM. Refs middling.

POSITIVES:
- EF backed, sold a company, CTO @ Simpl
- Strong technical mind
- Thoughtful and pragmatic
- Workspot scaled to $20M but flatlined after - Puneet mostly looked after tech, limited business focus
- Well networked in US, some good angel investments

CONCERNS:
- Thesis: AI to improve engineering documentation (maintain docs across codebase, enable agents to understand via context). Winner-take-all play with Entire.io and other strong competitors already. Wouldn't bet on this team to take them on.
- Customer backward-ness low - has strong opinions, will build good initial product but might stay blind to customer feedback on iterating it
- GTM muscle missing - his cofounders are two principal engineers. Should have hired complementary skills.
- Refs okayish

---

REFERENCE 1: Ashwini, VP @ Simpl (reported to him)

POSITIVES:
- Strong at hustling and getting things done (sets up war rooms, pushes integrations through)
- Good problem solver in execution-heavy situations
- Very effective networker; uses personal network (Flipkart, Swiggy, etc.) to unlock deals and integrations
- Can evolve/learn new areas (picked up GTM basics over ~8 months, went on pitches like Payne 3 to Myntra)
- Defends his team well and is an acceptable stakeholder manager
- Handles peer-level, technical/business counterparts well (e.g., JustPay relationship)
- In dev tooling / B2B technical space, she would trust him to build a good product

NEGATIVES:
- Weak business and customer understanding, especially in B2C and B2B2C; cannot really shape AOP, metrics, or strategy
- Tech leadership hiring quality mixed: only a few stellar hires (Vivek); rest of team rated ~5/10 vs ~9/10 benchmark
- Initial GTM understanding and positioning were poor; GTM is not his core capability
- As a founder, if he built an early team only of engineers without strong GTM counterpart, she would be worried
- If choosing where to angel invest, she'd pick Vivek over Puneet ("mad genius," better founding bet)

- Overall strong technical guy with good network

DETAILED MEETING NOTES FROM ASHWIN:

Background:
- Former VMware engineer (CTO office, 2007 IPO), part of 25-50 company acquisitions
- Left for EIR role at Redpoint Ventures (6 months) - "VC thing doesn't work for me - want action, not pontification"
- Founded Workspot (6-7 years as CTO) - Desktop-in-cloud for regulated industries, $20-25M revenue but couldn't scale against cloud vendors. Key learning: "Full stack always wins" - too dependent on partners
- 4 years at Simpl (joined during COVID) - Founder Nitya was Chandigarh roommate, scaled engineering team to 300+ engineers, always planned to start something again

Investment Track Record & Network:
- Invested in Balaji's CloudKnox (10x return to Microsoft acquisition)
- Balaji now starting Tika Security, wants to invest in Puneet's company
- Bloomberg Capital connection through VMware colleague
- Hosts security founder dinners/lunches in Palo Alto
- Thesis: "India is the place to invest in next 30 years"
- Keen to build deep tech companies in India
- Interest in robotics and physical AI long-term

EXOC Product Vision:
- Core problem: AI agents are stateless, real world is stateful
- Inspired by Matrix scene: "I know kung fu" instant knowledge download
- Building unified knowledge layer for SDLC
- Not just connectors (like Composio) but system of context
- Focus on software development lifecycle vs everything (like Glean)
- Target: Companies with hundreds of agents
- Companies will write own agents vs paying for every possible one
- Need centralized knowledge system for agent effectiveness

Technical Approach & Differentiation:
- PLG strategy with CLI-first product
- Free codebase scoring for agent readiness
- Identifies documentation gaps, performance-sensitive areas
- Captures implicit knowledge that senior engineers know
- Session tracking capability (similar to Entire.io)
- Monitors all agent interactions and reasoning
- ROI tracking for $200/month Claude subscriptions
- Built without using git (separate system of context)

Key Workflows:
- Onboarding: Learning paths for new developers
- Offboarding: Knowledge extraction questionnaires
- Planning: Voice-first interface for work dispatch

Competition & Market Position:
- Direct competitors: Entire.io (session capture, most futuristic), TESL (agent-facing documentation packets), GitHub/GitLab (too big, Microsoft-owned)
- Differentiation: Separate system vs git-based storage
- Git not designed for large chat session blobs
- MCP interface approach vs branch-based storage
- Future evolution: "Confluence for next Atlassian" - Knowledge layer as entry wedge, planning to replace Jira/Linear with voice-first workflow

Funding & Next Steps:
- Current status: 4-person team (3 co-founders + 1 engineer)
- All from Simpl, equity split 60/10/10, 10% SPC, 10% option pool
- $1M pre-seed from SPC completed, money still in bank
- Fundraising timeline: Wrap up before end-March US trip
- Target: $2.5-3M more, 15-20% dilution
- Some firms already doing aggressive diligence
- Product status: SPC founders testing, 1-2 friendly design partners, nothing in production yet

Ashwin's process: 2-3 days for thesis alignment, then portfolio founder introductions
